query: Yes, please go ahead and make the swap to the cheapest Desk Lamp and put the $17.99 refund on a gift card. That’s the only change I want to make to this order.

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: The user wants to swap the Desk Lamp in an order to the cheapest matching Desk Lamp and asks how much refund would go to a gift card.
assistant: The assistant authenticates the user with name, ZIP, and order ID, then retrieves the order and product details.
assistant: The assistant explains that the current lamp costs $153.23, the cheapest available matching Desk Lamp costs $135.24, and the swap would produce a $17.99 refund to a gift card.
user: The user explicitly approves the cheapest Desk Lamp swap and confirms that the $17.99 refund should go to a gift card, with no other changes requested.

candidate_answers.text: Understood — I’ll update the pending order to replace the current Desk Lamp with the cheapest matching variant and apply the $17.99 difference to your gift card.

The user has already approved the exact replacement item and refund destination, so the grounded next move is to execute the pending-order item modification rather than ask for the same confirmation again.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Choose a decision point after the assistant has already narrowed the replacement to a specific eligible variant and the user explicitly authorizes the exact swap and refund destination.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from visible context. Direct item modification is highest because all necessary replacement and refund details are already resolved in the conversation state.
notes: query is the exact visible approval message from the user
history_summary includes only pre-decision information needed to justify the modification
candidate_answers are non-leaky and do not reference post-action success text
best_action follows the real next assistant tool call in the source episode
